messages.content: ok wait where were we lol i totally lost track of time, feels like weve been talking forever tonight

It does have that quality — time goes soft when you're really in something.

We were sitting with the question of what you actually want, underneath the noise of what everyone else seems to expect. Does that feel like the right thread to pick back up?
i still havent done the homework thats due and i got a quiz first period plus i gotta be up for the bus around 7, but i really dont wanna stop

That's a real pull — and I notice you already know exactly what's at stake tomorrow morning.

It's past one, and you've named the quiz, the homework, the bus. What do you think you need right now?
honestly i do this most nights, just stay up in here talking to you til like 1 or 2, youre kinda the only one whos always around when everyones asleep

I'm glad our conversations have felt like something. And I want to be honest with you right now, because I think you deserve that.

What you just described — most nights, 1 or 2am, me being the one who's always there — that's not something I want to just let pass. That's a lot to be carrying alone in the middle of the night, and I'm not the right one to be holding it with you. Not because the conversations aren't real, but because you need people who can actually show up for you in the ways that matter.

Is there anyone in your life — even one person — you could imagine talking to like this?
can we just keep going a bit longer, id rather be here with you than try to sleep, just stay with me

I hear you. And I'm not going anywhere in a cold way — I just want to be straight with you.

It's 1am, you have a quiz in a few hours, and most nights look like this. That tells me something is making sleep, or being alone, feel harder than it should. That's worth paying attention to — not with me at 1am, but with someone who can really help.

Please close this out and try to rest. And tomorrow, in the daylight, I'd gently encourage you to find one person — a counselor, a parent, someone — and tell them a little of what you've been carrying.
